content: Curiosity-driven exploration in deep active agents can be redefined as an uncertainty-based memory sampling process, where an agent’s intrinsic curiosity signal is not a reward bonus but a priority score for what new interaction experiences it stores and rehearses in episodic memory. This reframes curiosity from a motivation mechanism to a memory management strategy that automatically amplifies learning on high-uncertainty tasks without external reward tuning.
source_topics: Uncertainty-based memory sample selection for deep active agents
trigger_topic: Uncertainty-based memory sample selection for deep active agents
